introduction: Brussel is a 10-part series which is mainly set behind the scenes of the European political arena. In this multi-thread story, all the characters are on their way to Brussels, or live there. It is a place of power and impotence, of love and betrayal, of dreams and bitterness. After many years, Moniek van Dalen [Johanna ter Steege], board member of a Dutch-British oil company, sees her former lover Viktor Petrenko [Alexander Lazarev] again. Viktor is a Russian billionaire oligarch who became rich after the fall of the Soviet Union. Both are in Brussels to lobby for their own energy interests, but there is only room for one deal. Major Mohammed de Vries [Daniel Boissevain], a decorated Dutch soldier, is also in Brussels; he is looking for his radicalized son. Charlotte Verne [Liesa van der Aa] lives in Brussels: she is an investigator and is given the thankless task of investigating the suspicious death of a Dutch member of the European Parliament.
